title: Freshness and completeness are independent
description: Recent data can still be incomplete (partial load) and complete data can be stale; both must be checked separately, and neither follows from job success.
body: Use when reasoning about whether a dataset is trustworthy. A pipeline reporting success guarantees neither: it can land a fresh-but-partial partition (timely, incomplete) or process nothing new while old data looks complete (complete, stale). Freshness measures recency of the newest data; completeness measures whether all expected rows arrived. Do not infer either from a green run. Actions: check freshness via latest-timestamp age and completeness via count reconciliation, independently. Validation signals include both a freshness and a reconciliation check passing. Risk boundary: assuming success implies good data is the classic silent-quality failure.
